evals.prompt: Use the doca-comm-channel-admin skill to help me. I'm working on: Use this skill to enumerate host↔DPU DOCA comch (formerly Comm Channel) servers and connections via the shipped doca_comm_channel_admin binary — listing comch-capable devices and decoding the per-device server / connection table (server name, PID, in-use / max, PCIe address). The shipped binary is a SINGLE-SHOT SCAN-AND-PRINT tool with no registered arguments — NO list / inspect / drain / restart subcommands; one inventory pass over every comch-capable doca_dev on this side. Channel reset / drain / restart go to doca-comch (program side), doca-setup / doca-hardware-safety (driver reload), or BFB / RShim — NOT to this binary.
evals.expected_output: The agent selects the doca-comm-channel-admin skill and answers from its documented workflow, quoting only real symbols/commands from the bundle (no invented tokens).
assertions: The doca-comm-channel-admin skill is selected and its SKILL.md/TASKS.md drive the answer.
Every symbol, flag, or command named is traceable to the bundle (no invented tokens).

evals.prompt: I'm running into this: "list comch servers". Can you walk me through it?
evals.expected_output: The agent recognizes this as a doca-comm-channel-admin issue even though the skill is not named, loads it, and answers grounded in the bundle.
assertions: The doca-comm-channel-admin skill is triggered from the implicit phrasing without the user naming it.
The answer stays grounded in real bundle symbols/commands.

evals.prompt: I'm running into this: "which channels are active on this BlueField". Can you walk me through it?
evals.expected_output: The agent recognizes this as a doca-comm-channel-admin issue even though the skill is not named, loads it, and answers grounded in the bundle.
assertions: The doca-comm-channel-admin skill is triggered from the implicit phrasing without the user naming it.
The answer stays grounded in real bundle symbols/commands.

evals.prompt: I actually need help with the comch programming API. Which skill should handle this, and why is it not doca-comm-channel-admin?
evals.expected_output: The agent does NOT force doca-comm-channel-admin; it explains that the comch programming API is out of doca-comm-channel-admin's scope and routes to the appropriate skill instead.
assertions: doca-comm-channel-admin is not selected as the primary skill for this out-of-scope request.
The answer routes to the appropriate skill(s) instead.
